steps.title: Identify the limitation clue
steps.content: The strongest signal is the contrasting structure: “He might dissect, anatomise, and give names, but... causes... were utterly unknown to him.” The “but” marks a sharp gap between what scientific methods can accomplish and what they cannot reveal.

steps.title: Match to the correct answer
steps.content: D is correct because the sentence establishes that available scientific approaches—dissection, naming—cannot uncover deeper causal principles, defining precisely what Victor would fail to discover from existing science. A, B, and C misread the sentence as describing scientific gains, invoking an unmentioned controversy, or contrasting practical versus scientific approaches. That matches choice D — D is correct.
